data.title: Materials Gain After Jobs Data - Materials Roundup
data.description:
Producers of metals and other raw materials rose after tepid jobs data and more salvos in a burgeoning trade war kept rate-cut hopes alive, despite a vow from Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell to remain patient.

"We expect that the labor market will continue to cool gradually in the months ahead, with downside risks due to government spending uncertainty," said economists at brokerage Jefferies. The Jefferies economists forecast three rate cuts this year, while cautioning that the Trump administration's unpredictable policies could trigger adjustments to the forecast.

A second round of tariffs on Chinese imports means that all goods from that nation will face at least a 20% levy at the U.S. border. China reacted in kind, levying tariffs on U.S. agricultural products, coal, energy and some manufactured goods.

Wang Yi, China's foreign minister, condemned Trump's moves as a failure to abide by trading norms, and vowed more retaliation.

Gold futures fell from record highs as traders rotated back into risky sectors from safe havens. Copper futures have strengthened recently due to the weakening dollar and optimism about Chinese economic activity.

Write to Rob Curran at rob.curran@dowjones.com

data.title: MGM Resorts International CFO Buys 10,000 Shares
data.description:
Reporter NameHalkyard Jonathan S
RelationshipCHIEF FINANCIAL OFFICER
TypePurchase
Amount$321,700
SEC FilingForm 4

Jonathan S Halkyard, Chief Financial Officer of MGM Resorts International, purchased 10,000 shares of the company's common stock at a price of $32.17 per share, totaling $321,700. Following this transaction, Halkyard directly owns 75,648 shares of MGM Resorts International.

SEC Filing: MGM Resorts International [ MGM ] - Form 4 - Mar. 07, 2025

data.title: Industrials Advance on Fed Hopes - Industrials Roundup
data.description:
Shares of industrial and transportation companies rose amid hopes that weak jobs data would spur the Federal Reserve to cut rates.

The economy added a seasonally adjusted 151,000 jobs in February, the Labor Department reported, slightly below the gain of 170,000 jobs economists polled by The Wall Street Journal expected to see.

Fed Chairman Jerome Powell said the central bank was waiting to see whether the Trump administration's trade and economic policies affected inflation and other economic trends.

The burgeoning trade war means the central bank is highly unlikely to alter its policy in the near term, according to most strategists.

Economists at brokerage Jefferies said a rate cut was likely in June, if the economy remains on its current trajectory.

French shipping magnate Rodolphe Saade, chief executive of CMA CGM, met President Trump at the White House on Thursday and pledged to invest $20 billion in the U.S. over the next four years.

Write to Rob Curran at rob.curran@dowjones.com

data.title: Health Care Flat Amid Deal Activity - Health Care Roundup
data.description:
Health-care companies were more or less flat amid deal activity.

Walgreens Boots Alliance rose after leveraged-buyout firm Sycamore Partners agreed to buy the global drug-store chain for $11.45 a share in cash, or about $10 billion, a 29% premium.

It was a respite for Walgreens after a punishing few years during which the historic U.S. drug-store chain struggled to incorporate the purchase of British rival Boots. In less than 10 years, Walgreens strategic slips reduced it from one of the world's most valuable companies to a buyout target. Appetite for higher risk health-care issues, such as

Write to Rob Curran at rob.curran@dowjones.com

data.title: Energy Gains With Oil on OPEC+ Hopes - Energy Roundup
data.description:
Shares of energy companies ticked up alongside oil futures after a Russian deputy prime minister reportedly said OPEC+ could reverse plans to increase production if prices remain under pressure.

President Trump said he is "strongly considering" imposing far-reaching sanctions and tariffs on Russia until a peace agreement is reached in the war in Ukraine, as his administration pushes for a peace deal.

Write to Rob Curran at rob.curran@dowjones.com

data.title: Avery Dennison Executive Chairman Sells Over $2.3 Million in Company Stock
data.description:
Reporter NameButier Mitchell R
RelationshipExecutive Chairman
TypeSell
Amount$2,358,387
SEC FilingForm 4

Mitchell R. Butier, Executive Chairman and Director at Avery Dennison Corp, sold 12,969 shares of Common Stock on March 6, 2025, in three separate transactions. The sales were conducted at weighted average prices of $181.2182, $182.3054, and $182.7773, totaling approximately $2,358,387. Following these transactions, Butier directly owns 303,331 shares of Avery Dennison Corp Common Stock and indirectly owns 4,222 shares through a Savings Plan.

SEC Filing: Avery Dennison Corp [ AVY ] - Form 4 - Mar. 07, 2025

data.title: Tech Advances, Recouping Some Losses After Broadcom Earnings - Tech Roundup
data.description:
Shares of technology companies rose, recouping some of their recent losses following strong earnings from one major chip maker.

Broadcom shares soared after the maker of chips for artificial-intelligence applications posted quarterly earnings ahead of Wall Street targets, reflecting 77% growth in AI revenue.

Nvidia and other major chip makers rose in a relief rally. The chip sector recouped some of the losses from Thursday's rout, triggered by a revenue shortfall reported by Broadcom rival Marvell Technologies.

Write to Rob Curran at rob.curran@dowjones.com

data.title: Financials Drop on Volatility Fears - Financials Roundup
data.description:
Shares of banks and other financial institutions fell amid concerns about interest rates and volatile equity markets.

Insurance giant Allianz is in exclusive talks to acquire fellow German insurance-industry services firm Viridium for more than $3 billion.

Leveraged-buyout firm Bain Capital will get a look at Insignia Financial's books after it sweetened its offer for the Australian wealth manager to about $2.12 billion, matching a bid from rival suitor CVC Capital.

Investment firm Blackstone closed on an $8 billion commercial real-estate debt fund, in the latest sign of a rebound in commercial property following a prolonged, pandemic-era slump in office occupancy.

Write to Rob Curran at rob.curran@dowjones.com

data.title: Dream Finders Homes Completes Acquisition of Cherry Creek Mortgage Through Its Wholly Owned Subsidiary, Jet HomeLoans
data.description:
(22:51 GMT) *Dream Finders Homes Completes Acquisition Of Cherry Creek Mortgage Through Its Wholly Owned Subsidiary, Jet HomeLoans >DFH
(22:51 GMT) Press Release: Dream Finders Homes Completes Acquisition of Cherry Creek Mortgage Through Its Wholly Owned Subsidiary, Jet HomeLoans

Dream Finders Homes Completes Acquisition of Cherry Creek Mortgage Through Its Wholly Owned Subsidiary, Jet HomeLoans

JACKSONVILLE, Fla.--(BUSINESS WIRE)--March 07, 2025--

Dream Finders Homes, Inc. (the "Company", "Dream Finders Homes", "Dream Finders" or "DFH") announced the acquisition of Cherry Creek Mortgage, LLC ("Cherry Creek") through its wholly owned subsidiary, Jet HomeLoans, LP ("Jet HomeLoans"). Cherry Creek is a Freddie Mac and GNMA-approved lender that specializes in the origination of mortgage loans to support new build construction lending. The acquisition was formally closed on March 4, 2025, marking a significant expansion in Jet HomeLoans' servicing capabilities.

Founded in 1986, Cherry Creek became one of the nation's preeminent mortgage lending platforms from their headquarters in Denver, Colorado. Under the leadership of founder Jeff May, Cherry Creek built a respected originator, securitizer, and servicer of conventional and government loans across the United States including reverse mortgages.

Kelley Hailstone, President of Jet HomeLoans, shared his thoughts on the acquisition: "We are thrilled to welcome the entire Cherry Creek Mortgage team to the Jet HomeLoans family. This partnership leverages Jet HomeLoans' existing retail production strength with Cherry Creek's robust expertise in servicing along with all necessary industry approvals. The result is a stronger, more diversified company that will deliver a superior experience for our growing customer base. Most importantly, these complementary capabilities will allow Jet HomeLoans to maximize affordability for our Dream Finders homebuyers."

Jet HomeLoans was advised on the transaction by Sterling Point Advisors, Milliman, Inc.'s Mortgage Solutions team, and McGlinchey Stafford PLLC, a law firm based in New Orleans, LA.

About Dream Finders Homes, Inc.

Dream Finders Homes is a homebuilder based in Jacksonville, Florida. Dream Finders Homes builds single-family homes throughout the Southeast, Mid-Atlantic and Midwest, including Florida, Texas, Tennessee, North Carolina, South Carolina, Georgia, Colorado, Arizona, and the Washington, D.C. metropolitan area, which comprises Northern Virginia and Maryland. Through its wholly owned subsidiaries, DFH also provides mortgage financing as well as title services to homebuyers. Dream Finders Homes achieves its industry-leading growth and returns by maintaining an asset-light homebuilding model. For more information, please visit www.dreamfindershomes.com.

About Jet HomeLoans, LP

Jet HomeLoans is a Florida-based HUD / FHA / VA / USDA approved, independent mortgage banker licensed in 13 states. Jet HomeLoans originated over $2 billion in mortgage loans in 2024. Jet HomeLoans is a wholly owned subsidiary of Dream Finders Homes, Inc.

Forward-Looking Statements

This press release includes forward-looking statements regarding future events which include, but are not limited to, market conditions, possible or assumed future results of operations, expected benefits of the Cherry Creek acquisition and statements regarding the Company's strategies and expectations as they relate to market opportunities and growth. All forward-looking statements are based on management's beliefs as well as assumptions made by and the information currently available to management. These statements reflect managements' current views with respect to future events and are subject to various risks, uncertainties and assumptions, which are discussed in Dream Finders Homes' Annual Report on Form 10-K for the year ended December 31, 2024, subsequently filed Form 10-Qs and other filings with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission. Dream Finders Homes undertakes no obligation to update or revise any forward-looking statement except as may be required by applicable law.

View source version on businesswire.com: https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20250307415969/en/

CONTACT: Investor Contact: investors@dreamfindershomes.com

Media Contact: mediainquiries@dreamfindershomes.com

data.title: Dream Finders Homes Completes Acquisition Of Cherry Creek Mortgage Through Its Wholly Owned Subsidiary, Jet HomeLoans >DFH
Consumer Cos Flat on Luxury Slide - Consumer Roundup
data.description:
Consumer companies were more or less flat amid renewed signs of distress in the luxury niche.

Shares of Italian high-end fashion firm Salvatore Ferragamo tumbled as a rebranding effort faltered.

The sales disappointment heightened concerns that younger consumers in the West were favoring "fast fashion" and casual clothes over premium brands.

Shares of Louis Vuitton owner LVMH, Gucci parent Kering, Burberry and Cartier owner Richemont fell in sympathy. Historic Canadian chain Hudson's Bay is set to file for bankruptcy.

Write to Rob Curran at rob.curran@dowjones.com

data.title: Communciations Services Climb, on Rotation Back to Risk - Communications Services Roundup
data.description:
Communications services companies rose, as traders rotated back into high-risk sectors.

Shares of Universal Music rose after the record label behind Taylor Swift posted subscription revenue growth that was ahead of consensus estimates.

ESPN anchor Stephen Smith is getting a raise, the prominent commentator and executive producer of the sports network's morning sports talk show "First Take," signed a new five-year deal with the network worth more than $100 million.

Write to Rob Curran at rob.curran@dowjones.com

data.title: DoorDash, Williiams-Sonoma, and TKO Stock Get Added to the S&P 500 — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Mackenzie Tatananni and Anita Hamilton

DoorDash, World Wrestling Entertainment owner TKO Group Holdings, and home goods company Williams-Sonoma were added to the S&P 500 as part of a quarterly reshuffling of the index. Borgwarner, Teleflex, Celanese, and FMC were removed. The changes will take effect on March 24.

This is a breaking story. Check back for updates.

S&P Dow Jones Indices makes additions and deletions to the index, which tracks the 500 largest publicly listed companies by market capitalization, on a quarterly basis. The changes will take effect after the close on March 21; they're generally announced two weeks in advance.

Barclays analysts have identified seven companies within their coverage as potential candidates for S&P 500 inclusion. The largest is Interactive Brokers, a Connecticut-based brokerage firm with a market capitalization of $80.7 billion.

Coinbase and Ares Management are next on the list, with $54.4 billion and $50 billion market caps, respectively. Other possible candidates include Robinhood, LPL Financial, Blue Owl Capital, and Tradeweb Markets.

Though not mentioned in the Barclays report, investors have their eyes on AppLovin. With a market cap of $88.3 billion, the mobile technology company is the largest of all candidates that meet the S&P Dow Jones Indices criteria, according to Dow Jones Market Data. Another strong contender is DoorDash, with a market cap of $75.7 billion.

As of Thursday's close, FMC, Celanese, and Caesars Entertainment were among the lowest in market cap in the S&P 500, according to Dow Jones Market Data, putting them at risk of removal.

S&P Global announced the addition of Apollo Global Management and Workday to the S&P 500 index last quarter, replacing Qorvo and Amentum Holdings, which were moved to the S&P SmallCap 600.

A company must meet a lengthy list of criteria to be considered. For starters, it must be U.S.-domiciled and listed on one of 10 eligible U.S. exchanges, excluding the OTC Bulletin Board and pink sheets.

Moreover, its market cap must be greater than $14.5 billion, with the primary listing's float greater than 50% of the market cap. The company's sum of GAAP net income over the four most recent consecutive quarters must also be positive, as well as the most recent quarter.

Analysts led by Benjamin Budish noted that stocks selected for index inclusion typically see gains of 7% or more in the period ahead of their first day of trading in the index, "making Friday's event a potential catalyst for the stocks' activity."

However, the stocks sell off by an average of 1.3% in the first week following index inclusion and are down around 1% in the following four weeks, the analysts wrote.

In addition, stocks selected for addition have sold off on average by 16 base points from the announcement to the close of the rebalance day, "and have also seen slightly negative returns in the first day and week following the rebalance," the analysts wrote.

Write to Mackenzie Tatananni at mackenzie.tatananni@barrons.com and Anita Hamilton at anita.hamilton@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Peloton Interactive's Chief Content Officer Sells Shares
data.description:
Reporter NameCotter Jennifer Cunningham
RelationshipChief Content Officer
TypeSell
Amount$1,208,228
SEC FilingForm 4

Jennifer Cunningham Cotter, Chief Content Officer of Peloton Interactive, sold 187,238 shares of Class A Common Stock on March 6, 2025, at a weighted average price of $6.4529 per share, totaling $1,208,228. Following the transaction, Cotter directly owns 100,269 shares of the company. The sale was conducted under a Rule 10b5-1 trading plan adopted on December 5, 2024.

SEC Filing: PELOTON INTERACTIVE, INC. [ PTON ] - Form 4 - Mar. 07, 2025

data.title: ONESPAWORLD Director Leonard I Fluxman Sells 4,401 Shares
data.description:
Reporter NameFluxman Leonard I
RelationshipDirector
TypeSell
Amount$84,960
SEC FilingForm 4

Leonard I Fluxman, serving as Director and noted with the title 'See Remarks', sold 4,401 Common Shares of ONESPAWORLD HOLDINGS Ltd. The transaction occurred on March 5, 2025, with a weighted average selling price of $19.3048 per share, totaling $84,960. Following this transaction, Fluxman directly owns 1,438,070 Common Shares and indirectly owns 285,338 Common Shares through Fluxman Family Holdings LLC.

SEC Filing: ONESPAWORLD HOLDINGS Ltd [ OSW ] - Form 4 - Mar. 07, 2025

data.title: Match Group President Gary Swidler Sells Over $7.9 Million in Company Stock
data.description:
Reporter NameSwidler Gary
RelationshipPresident
TypeSell
Amount$7,960,085
SEC FilingForm 4

Gary Swidler, serving as President of Match Group, reported selling a total of 242,209 shares of Match Group's common stock on March 7, 2025, for a total sale amount of $7,960,085. The sales were conducted at weighted average prices of $33.4001 and $32.7988, as detailed in two separate transactions. Following these transactions, Swidler directly owns 1,690 shares of Match Group's common stock.

SEC Filing: Match Group, Inc. [ MTCH ] - Form 4 - Mar. 07, 2025

data.title: Dorman Products SVP, CIO Donna M. Long Sells 742 Shares
data.description:
Reporter NameLong Donna M.
RelationshipSVP, CIO
TypeSell
Amount$96,742
SEC FilingForm 4

Donna M. Long, SVP, CIO of Dorman Products, sold 742 shares of Common Stock on March 6, 2025, at weighted average prices ranging from $129.0572 to $133.0514, resulting in a total sale amount of $96,742. Following these transactions, Long directly owns 19,893 shares of Dorman Products. The sales were conducted in multiple transactions at varying prices.

SEC Filing: Dorman Products, Inc. [ DORM ] - Form 4 - Mar. 07, 2025
